title: CryptoAnomalyClassification
description: LLM classification verdict for a single anomaly episode. Loaded by crypto_analyser.llm_client.LLMClient._load_schema and enforced via OpenAI-compatible structured output with strict=true. See ADR-0002 (severity derived) and ADR-0003 (classification rule as prompt rubric).

event_reference.description: Correlation key set by the caller (Task 18) — typically the episode onset_ts. Echoed back by the LLM; back-filled by llm_client.ClassificationResult.from_dict if the LLM omits it.

classification.description: explained_derivatives: funding magnitude or OI change breaches the rubric thresholds (config anomaly_detection.derivatives_thresholds). explained_news: retrieved news context (Run B only) provides a credible explanation that derivatives did not. unexplained: derivatives quiet AND no credible news available. insufficient_data: one or more derivatives feature values are null.

severity.description: Derived (NOT LLM-emitted) from the episode's peak |Z|: low (>=2.5), medium (>=3.0), high (>=4.0), extreme (>=5.0). Computed by Task 14 and stored on each episode record; Task 18 reads it verbatim. See ADR-0002.

confidence.description: LLM-reported confidence that the chosen classification is well-supported by the cited feature values. Anchored to evidence (see system prompt), not a free-floating self-rating. Used downstream by the Milestone-2 Bull/Bear/Judge debate layer.

rationale.description: Substantive prose defending the classification against the cited feature values. References specific funding rate / OI numbers from the input. Run B variants additionally reference why retrieved news does or does not explain the move. Ragas (faithfulness, answer_relevancy) evaluates this field.

news_relevance.description: Run B only. Free-form assessment of whether the retrieved news context explains the price move. Null in Run A (no news in scope).
